Name: Basílica de Nuestra Señora del Pilar
Tips: Enormous Baroque basilica on the Ebro — Spain's most visited pilgrimage site. Go at dusk: cross to Puente de Piedra for the iconic view of the basilica reflecting in the river. Evening illumination extraordinary.

Name: La Aljafería Palace
Address: C/ de los Diputados s/n Zaragoza
Tips: 11th-century Moorish palace comparable to the Alhambra but almost no crowds. Extraordinary Islamic filigree stucco, carved arches, and later Gothic and Renaissance additions. Allow 1.5 hours minimum — do not rush.

Name: La Seo Cathedral (Catedral del Salvador)
Tips: Built over a Roman forum and Moorish mosque — layers visible. The Mudéjar apse is one of the finest in Spain. World-class tapestry museum inside. More beautiful inside than El Pilar.

Name: Museo del Foro de Caesaraugusta
Tips: Excavated Roman forum preserved underground with excellent audiovisuals. Cool, uncrowded, genuinely fascinating. Zaragoza (Caesaraugusta) was one of Rome's most important Iberian cities.

Name: Puente de Piedra
Tips: 15th-century stone bridge over the Ebro — walk to the middle at dusk for the classic view of El Pilar's tiled domes reflected in the river. The signature postcard image of Zaragoza.

Name: Plaza del Pilar
Tips: The full scale of the basilica from the square is extraordinary — 156m long, 67m wide. Best seen in daylight. The square links El Pilar and La Seo cathedrals.

Name: Mercado Central de Zaragoza
Tips: 1903 Modernist iron market with stained-glass roof. Beautiful architecture. Browse on arrival — closed by evening. Good for orientation and snacks.
